title: Disinfo Update: Civil Society Mobilises | Council of Europe Actions | MFF Budgets
snippet: The EU DisinfoLab's fortnightly newsletter features recent research co-authored by Fabio Giglietto and Giada Marino in its Research and Platform Governance segment. The featured work examines how partisan alignment, journalistic quality, and algorithmic governance alter visibility and audience reach on Facebook.
description: An EU DisinfoLab newsletter featuring Fabio Giglietto's co-authored research on how partisan alignment, journalistic quality, and algorithmic governance influence content visibility on Facebook.

title: How Facebook's Algorithmic Tweaks Affected Engagement with News URLs over Time
snippet: A liveblogged summary from the ICA 2026 conference in Cape Town, reporting on Fabio Giglietto's presentation. His study analyzed how US partisan alignment, journalistic quality (via NewsGuard ratings), and Meta's algorithmic adjustments collectively shaped the reach of over 130,000 news URLs on Facebook.
description: A detailed blog post covering Fabio Giglietto's presentation at the ICA 2026 conference, analyzing how Meta's algorithmic tweaks impact user engagement with news URLs on Facebook.

title: socialmediaandsociety.org
description: An announcement for the International Conference on Social Media & Society listing Professor Fabio Giglietto as an expert panelist discussing future pathways for social media research following the closure of CrowdTangle.

title: Indagine urbinate: "Così l’algoritmo influenza il voto" - Il Resto del Carlino
description: News article from Il Resto del Carlino

title: Algoritmi e democrazia, il caso Meta secondo lo studio di UniUrb
snippet: Press coverage detailing the political and institutional impact of Fabio Giglietto’s paper 'A Pretty Blunt Approach.' The article notes that following the study's revelations on Meta's algorithmic biases, Italian regulator AGCOM invited Giglietto for a hearing and the European Commission launched inquiries.
description: Press coverage from Vivere Urbino detailing the significant institutional impact of Fabio Giglietto's study on Meta's algorithms, which prompted an AGCOM hearing and European Commission inquiries.

title: lumsanews.it
description: An article covering Professor Fabio Giglietto's research analyzing over 2.5 million Facebook posts, focusing on how political extremist pages bypassed Meta's filters to gain visibility during elections.

title: Facebook premia gli estremisti: la ricerca dell'Università di Urbino - Lumsanews
Meno contenuti politici nei feed, la stretta di Meta è stata meno efficace contro gli estremisti - Il Sole 24 ORE
description: News article from Il Sole 24 ORE

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: Coverage by Open online newspaper summarizing Fabio Giglietto’s research on how Meta’s political content filter systematically decreased the organic reach of elected Italian parliamentarians by over 70%, while allowing fringe political groups to flourish, sparking a push for bipartisan platform regulation.
description: An article by Open online newspaper detailing Fabio Giglietto's research on how Meta's political content filter significantly reduced the organic reach of Italian parliamentarians while boosting fringe groups.

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme - Open
